channel.title: Tech Insights
channel.description: Daily curated news on AI, developer tools, and the tech industry

item.title: I used Google Sheets as a database and it actually worked
item.description: I know. You read that title and thought "that's a terrible idea." Hear me out. I needed a Gantt chart for my team. We tried Notion, Monday.com, even a brief moment of insanity with MS Project....

item.title: 30% of Developers Think AI Will Replace Them
item.description: A web developer with five years of experience posted one line on Reddit after trying the latest Claude Max: "I feel increasingly irrelevant." The thread exploded. Thousands of comments. Heated...

item.title: Anthropic's $60B IPO Bet: What October Means for AI
item.description: $1 billion to $19 billion in annualized revenue. Fourteen months. That is the growth curve Anthropic is now trying to price on the public market. Bloomberg reported on March 27 that Anthropic —...

item.title: Anthropic Beat the Pentagon in Court — Here's Why It Matters
item.description: $200 million. That's the size of the contract Anthropic signed with the Pentagon in July 2025. Seven months later, the same government that hired them branded them a national security threat. Two...

item.title: Claude Code Auto Mode: No More Pressing Y
item.description: I counted once. During a 45-minute refactoring session with Claude Code, I pressed "y" to approve permissions 87 times. That's roughly once every 30 seconds. I wasn't coding. I was babysitting a...

item.title: Claude Now Controls Your Mac From Your iPhone
item.description: I sent "review the latest PR and leave comments" from my iPhone while waiting for coffee. By the time I sat down at my desk, Claude had already run git diff, analyzed 340 lines of changes across 6...

item.title: Claude's Paid Subscribers Are Skyrocketing
item.description: Here's a number that stopped me cold: Anthropic's annualized revenue hit $19 billion. That's 1,167% year-over-year growth. For context, the company earned its first dollar in revenue roughly three...

item.title: AI Fatigue for Developers: Managing Cognitive Overload from Code Assistants
item.description: The constant stream of suggestions from AI code assistants is creating a new kind of mental tax. The problem isn't prompt engineering or model accuracy. It's a deeper issue of control and focus,...

item.title: Reducing False Positives: Addressing Non-Exploitable CVEs in Hardened Kubernetes Environments with Vulnerability Scanners
item.description: Introduction: Addressing False Positives in Kubernetes Vulnerability Scanning In Kubernetes environments fortified with security measures, traditional vulnerability scanners such as Trivy and...

item.title: What makes a good MCP tool definition (and what makes a terrible one)
item.description: MCP tools are simple in theory: you define a function, describe what it does, and AI models call it when relevant. In practice, the quality of your tool definition determines whether your AI is...

item.title: Bluesky leans into AI with Attie, an app for building custom feeds
item.description: Bluesky’s new app Attie uses AI to help people build custom feeds the open social networking protocol atproto.

item.title: Stanford study outlines dangers of asking AI chatbots for personal advice
item.description: While there’s been plenty of debate about AI sycophancy, a new study by Stanford computer scientists attempts to measure how harmful that tendency might be.

item.title: Suno leans into customization with v5.5
item.description: Suno just released one of its biggest updates yet with v5.5 of its AI music model. Where previous updates focused mostly on improving fidelity and creating more natural vocals, v5.5 is about...

item.title: Elon Musk’s last co-founder reportedly leaves xAI
item.description: All but two of Musk's 11 xAI co-founders departed before this week.

item.title: The best deals we’ve found from Amazon’s Big Spring Sale (so far)
item.description: Amazon loves to manufacture an event. March is historically a dry spell for deals; however, with Amazon’s third annual Big Spring Sale, which runs through March 31st, the retail behemoth is hoping...

item.title: Iran-Linked Hackers Breach FBI Director’s Personal Email, Hit Stryker With Wiper Attack
item.description: Threat actors with ties to Iran successfully broke into the personal email account of Kash Patel, the director of the U.S. Federal Bureau of Investigation (FBI), and leaked a cache of photos and...

item.title: SteelSeries’ feature-packed Nova Pro Wireless headset is $80 off
item.description: The SteelSeries Arctis Nova Pro Wireless came out almost four years ago, yet it’s still one of the best gaming headsets you can buy. Turns out, it’s not easy for other companies to duplicate all...

item.title: Anthropic’s Claude popularity with paying consumers is skyrocketing
item.description: Estimates for total Claude consumer users are all over the map (we've seen figures ranging from 18 million to 30 million). Anthropic hasn't disclosed this data, but a spokesperson did tell...

item.title: Why can’t TikTok identify AI generated ads when I can?
item.description: I've been struggling to tell whether the ads appearing in my TikTok feeds have been made with generative AI tools. As someone who spends a great deal of time scrutinizing images and videos for the...

item.title: Meta’s legal defeat could be a victory for children, or a loss for everyone
item.description: Is social media not just bad, but illegally bad? Should tech companies pay for making it that way? According to two US juries - and no shortage of outside commentary - the answer to both questions...

item.title: A classic Zelda-style adventure, but a lot more cozy
item.description: The Legend of Zelda games are grand adventures, but they can also be very comforting, with quaint villages to explore and warm landscapes to take in. Under The Island takes that idea a step...

item.title: Here’s what Verge readers are buying during Amazon’s Big Spring Sale
item.description: We have a pretty good understanding of which deals Verge readers will gravitate toward. After all, what products and services resonate with our audience — and the quality of the deal itself — are...

item.title: Why OpenAI killed Sora
item.description: On Tuesday morning, everything was business as usual at OpenAI. By the end of the day, the company had announced that it would scrap its video-generation app, Sora, and reverse plans for video...

item.title: The must-have app for frequent flyers
item.description: Hi, friends! Welcome to Installer No. 121, your guide to the best and Verge-iest stuff in the world. (If you're new here, welcome, good luck in the Elite Eight, and also you can read all the old...

item.title: Oppo made the best foldable phone, again
item.description: There are always a few reasons people give for not buying a foldable phone. Some - price, battery life, camera quality - are mostly a matter of manufacturers balancing spec sheets to offset the...

item.title: Citrix NetScaler Under Active Recon for CVE-2026-3055 (CVSS 9.3) Memory Overread Bug
item.description: A recently disclosed critical security flaw impacting Citrix NetScaler ADC and NetScaler Gateway is witnessing active reconnaissance activity, according to Defused Cyber and watchTowr. The...

item.title: A woman’s uterus has been kept alive outside the body for the first time
item.description: “Think of this as a human body,” says Javier González. In front of me is essentially a metal box on wheels. Standing at around a meter in height, it reminds me of a stainless-steel counter in a...

item.title: TA446 Deploys DarkSword iOS Exploit Kit in Targeted Spear-Phishing Campaign
item.description: Proofpoint has disclosed details of a targeted email campaign in which threat actors with ties to Russia are leveraging the recently disclosed DarkSword exploit kit to target iOS devices. The...

item.title: CISA Adds CVE-2025-53521 to KEV After Active F5 BIG-IP APM Exploitation
item.description: The U.S. Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA) on Friday added a critical security flaw impacting F5 BIG-IP Access Policy Manager (APM) to its Known Exploited Vulnerabilities...

item.title: STADLER reshapes knowledge work at a 230-year-old company
item.description: Learn how STADLER uses ChatGPT to transform knowledge work, saving time and accelerating productivity across 650 employees.
